item.title: Warsh Says He’ll ‘Absolutely Not’ Be President’s Sock Puppet
item.description: Kevin Warsh said he will be an independent actor as head of the Federal Reserve.

item.title: UK Proposes Law Banning Mobile Phones at Schools in England
item.description: The UK government is set to ban students from using phones at schools in England, formalizing existing guidance for teachers as it looks to cut the amount of time young people spend online and on social media.

item.title: Canadian Telecom Rogers Goes Into Tailspin as Price War Heats Up
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc. has gone from the top performer among Canada’s three big telecommunications companies to the sector’s biggest drag, as a wireless price war threatens its premium valuation.

item.title: MUFG Bank Seeks More Acquisitions After $4.3 Billion India Deal
item.description: MUFG Bank Ltd. is looking for opportunities to make further acquisitions or investments in Asia and elsewhere, according to the new head of the lending unit at Japan’s largest banking group.

item.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Stays at a Trickle of Iran-Aligned Ships
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely devoid of commercial traffic on Tuesday as Iran continues enforcing a renewed closure of the waterway, leaving only a trickle of small vessels passing through. Most have ties to Tehran but are staying local.

item.title: Trump Urges Judge Not to Let Dimon ‘Escape’ JPMorgan Lawsuit
item.description: Donald Trump’s lawyer said JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon can’t “escape liability” for allegedly blacklisting the president after Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Warsh Says Fed Needs New Framework for Addressing Inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the US central bank, said the Federal Reserve needed a new framework for dealing with persistent inflation, without offering more specifics.

item.title: Brad Jacobs on His Big Bet on Building Insulation
item.description: The famed dealmaker on his latest acquisition.

item.title: Tiny Iran-Linked Tanker Tests US Blockade With Arabian Sea Move
item.description: A tiny tanker that’s reportedly transporting liquefied gas from Iran is testing a US blockade of the Islamic Republic’s shipping.

item.title: Odd Lots: QXO’s Brad Jacobs on His Big Insulation Bet (Podcast)
item.description: He’s done it again. On Sunday night, building supply company QXO announced that it would be acquiring TopBuild for $17 billion. TopBuild sells and installs insulation for both the residential and commercial markets. For Brad Jacobs, the CEO of QXO, this is just the latest in a lifetime of deals he’s made. In fact, he’s made over 500 deals in his life across numerous public companies that he’s founded, most of which have XO somewhere in the ticker. Brad’s companies all tend to be highly focused o

item.title: Ugandan Banks Balk at New ‘Foreign Agents’ Registration Law
item.description: Ugandan banks are seeking an exemption from proposed legislation requiring all businesses and individuals that receive funds from outside the country to be registered, arguing that the measure will negatively impact their ability to raise capital.

item.title: Le Pen’s Energy Plan for France Called a ‘Disaster’ by Engie CEO
item.description: A plan by Marine Le Pen’s far-right party to scrap renewables and the energy regulator would be a disaster for France, the CEO of giant utility Engie SA said, in one of the sharpest rebukes from a business leader ahead of next year’s presidential election.

item.title: Riskiest Junk Debt Is Lagging as Investors Continue to Fret About Software Firms’ AI Future
item.description: Investors, worried about artificial intelligence disrupting software companies, are skirting the most-troubled US junk debt even as they embrace risk in other markets.

item.title: US Natural Gas Ticks Down on Mild Weather, Growing Surplus
item.description: US natural gas futures fell slightly as weather forecasts shifted warmer, indicating slightly lower demand for the heating and power-plant fuel. Expectations that domestic stockpiles will move higher above the five-year average may also be putting pressure on prices.

item.title: Saudi IT Firm to Press Ahead with IPO Despite War Uncertainty
item.description: Saudi Arabian information technology services firm Dar Al Balad Business Solutions Co. is pressing ahead with plans to list on the kingdom’s stock exchange despite the disruptions caused by the regional conflict.

item.title: US Pending Home Sales Climbed in March on Improving Supply
item.description: Pending sales of previously owned US homes climbed for a second month in March as a pickup in inventory helped mitigate higher borrowing costs.

item.title: Treasuries Weaken Before Warsh Testifies for Fed Chair Role
item.description: Treasuries fell as strong economic data prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations ahead of testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

item.title: BMO, Teck, TC Energy CEOs to Advise Carney on US Strategy on New Panel
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is turning to some of Canada’s most prominent business executives, including Bank of Montreal’s Darryl White, to give him advice on how to handle Canada’s economic relationship with the US.

item.title: German Pension VBL Shifts €7 Billion Housing Portfolio Into Fund
item.description: German public-sector pension fund Versorgungsanstalt des Bundes und der Länder, or VBL, is transferring its residential property portfolio worth around €7 billion ($8.2 billion) into a new fund structure.

item.title: Billionaire Fredriksen’s Firm Faces $1 Billion Claim After Failed Fraud Trial
item.description: Two former executives at billionaire John Fredriksen’s oil trading firm sued for about $1 billion in over allegations that the shipping tycoon’s failed “vindictive” lawsuit brought them to the “brink of financial ruin.”

item.title: Core Scientific Adds to AI Junk-Bond Wave With $3.3 Billion Deal
item.description: Core Scientific Inc. is looking to raise $3.3 billion from a junk-bond sale, joining a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Polish Arms Maker Set for Record Sales on Defense Spending Spree
item.description: Poland’s biggest defense group is targeting another record year as the European Union’s largest land force ramps up spending to counter threats from Russia and tap into new EU funding.

item.title: Russia Set to Ramp Up Oil Flows as Impact of Drone Strikes Fades
item.description: Moscow has resumed loading operations at key ports, setting the stage for a rebound in exports

item.title: AI is Already Finding Thousands of Software Flaws, Even Before Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: A bug-tracking program found 200 serious issues in roughly a week.

item.title: Ireland Trims Economic-Growth Projections on Iran Conflict
item.description: Ireland cut this year’s economic-growth projection, citing the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Switch Lands $2.6 Billion of Bank Pledges to Power Data Centers
item.description: Switch Inc. gathered $2.6 billion of bank pledges to help the data-center operator procure electricity, a mounting challenge for the booming industry and one of its biggest costs.

item.title: Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh to Testify at Confirmation Hearing
item.description: Kevin Warsh Photographer: Tierney L. Cross/Bloomberg

item.title: Flurry of US IPOs Race to Tap Market Ahead of SpaceX Debut
item.description: The US IPO market has gone from desolate to bustling in a matter of weeks, with companies looking to raise as much as $17.3 billion this month alone and capitalize on stocks’ resilience.

item.title: US Retail Sales Surge by Most in a Year as Spending Extends Beyond Gas
item.description: US retail sales soared in March by the most in a year, suggesting consumers continued to spend on a wide array of merchandise despite a surge in gasoline prices sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: SEC Monitoring ‘Emerging Pressures’ in Private Credit Space
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission is closely monitoring the “emerging pressures” in the private credit market as redemption requests persist amid rising default-rate projections, Chairman Paul Atkins said in prepared remarks.
